text:
[residual] A push under --push triggering a git credential prompt/failure must surface as a halt, not hang — that is failure-recovery (relevant), not an auth-model gap.
[residual] Auto-merge could write onto an unintended base branch — covered by data-integrity/boundary-edge (relevant), not an authz model.
[residual] Irreversible merge/remove leave only an in-memory/stdout ledger — durable-record concern routed to observability (relevant).
[residual] A crafted wi-id reaching git argv is option-injection — owned by injection/input-validation (relevant).
[residual] If a halt reason echoes subprocess output with an author email it surfaces in the ledger — generic stderr scrubbing (secret-exposure, relevant), not a PII pipeline.
[residual] None identified — neither collects nor transmits regulated data; push goes to a remote the user already controls under existing policy.
[residual] A slow merge-back holding the worktree lock could approach the reused 30s deadline — exercised by concurrency-ordering (relevant), not new clock logic.
[close_reason] Local single-machine dev CLI; no authentication path added or touched, runs as invoking user. Only credential interaction is git's own push auth, reused unchanged.
[close_reason] No access-control model in this tool — single-user local git, all ops run with invoker's permissions. No who-may/may-not surface.
[close_reason] No security audit/non-repudiation requirement for a local dev tool; no multiple parties, no tamper-evidence need.
[close_reason] Local tool over a self-named WI list; no untrusted actor, no replay/bulk/scraping surface. Inputs from the operator.
[close_reason] Handles wi-ids/branch names/refs only; no PII collected, logged, or transmitted. Merge-commit author identity is pre-existing git state.
[close_reason] Local git dev tooling; no personal-data processing, no jurisdictional/consent/retention surface. No GDPR/compliance regime applies.
[close_reason] Driver introduces no time/clock dependence. Lone time logic (withWorktreeLock wall-clock 30s, worktree.ts:183,247) is reused unchanged, not net-new.
